description:
You are a propulsion engineer designing a LEO orbit-raising maneuver. Quantify the gravity loss penalty for using finite-duration burns compared to an ideal impulsive Hohmann transfer. Using the spacecraft and thruster specifications in ~/Desktop/orbit_raise_specs.txt, create a GMAT script implementing a spacecraft in a 400 km circular orbit performing a transfer to an 800 km circular orbit. The script must compare a two-burn impulsive Hohmann transfer against a finite burn transfer using a ChemicalThruster (400 N thrust, 316 s Isp, 80 kg fuel mass). Save the GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/gravity_loss_mission.script and a report to ~/GMAT_output/gravity_loss_report.txt containing the following lines (where <value> is the numerical result):
impulsive_dv1_m_s: <value>
impulsive_dv2_m_s: <value>
impulsive_total_dv_m_s: <value>
impulsive_final_sma_km: <value>
finite_dv1_eff_m_s: <value>
finite_dv2_eff_m_s: <value>
finite_total_dv_m_s: <value>
finite_final_sma_km: <value>
gravity_loss_m_s: <value>
gravity_loss_percent: <value>
Note: gravity_loss_m_s is the difference (finite_total_dv - impulsive_total_dv). Login: username=ga, password=password123
